rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF FRANKLIN GOLDBERG AND JUNA GOLDBERG VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Motion And Otion To Compel And For Sanctions; And Amended Memo Of P&A
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 7, 2012, Line 3, PLAINTIFFs ESTATE OF FRANKLIN GOLDBERG AND JUNA GOLDBERG, JUNA GOLDBERG (Amended) Notice Of Motion To Compel And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Trent Thornley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER HAMILTON VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO FIRE DEPARTMENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 7, 2012, Line 8. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff's first four causes of action (violations of Firefighters Procedural Bill of Rights Act) fail because the undisputed evidence demonstrates that plaintiff is not a firefighter within the meaning of Government Code section 3251(a). That section makes a distinction between a firefighter and a paramedic and covered the latter only when a paramedic is also a firefighter. Per section 3251(a) and common parlance, a firefighter is a person whose job is to suppress fires. Plaintiff does not and has not had such a job. Plaintiff's fifth cause of action fails because it is a common law claim and defendant's liability is determined by statute. Moreover, plaintiff presents no evidence that defendant's conduct disrupted plaintiff's relationship with a third party. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID LO et al VS. DANIEL S. LEE AKA DAN LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Expunge Plaintiffs' Notice Of Pendency Of Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 7, 2012, Line 10. DEFENDANT DANIEL S. LEE AKA DAN LEE, DSL HOLDINGS, LLC AND JUNKO TANAKA'S Notice Of Motion To Expunge Plaintiffs' Notice Of Pendency Of Action. Granted. The seventh cause of action for fraudulent conveyance in the Second Amended Complaint is not a real property claim within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure 405.4. Plaintiffs allege that defendant fraudulently transferred assets and money, not real property. The case law relied on by plaintiff is distinguishable from this action because that case law involved the fraudulent transfer of real property. Defendant's request for attorney's fees is denied because there was substantial justification for plaintiffs' position. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BANC OF AMERICA LEASING & CAPITAL, LLC VS. BRADSHAW & ASSOCIATES, A PROFESSIONAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 7, 2012, Line 25. CROSS DEFENDANT OCE FINANCIAL SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied and the alternative motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the fifth through eleventh causes of action and denied as to the first through fourth causes of action. Regarding the first through fourth causes of action, OFSI has not satisfied its burden of production to show that the Lease Agreement was a finance lease. Even if OFSI had satisfied its burden, there is a triable issue whether the Lease Agreement was unconscionable. The fifth and sixth causes of action fail because they are duplicative of the fourth cause of action. The seventh, eighth, tenth and eleventh causes of action fail because, as an assignee, Banc of America stepped into the shoes of OFSI. A cause of action for indemnity or contribution does not lie against an assignor after an asserted indemnitee settles its claims with the assignee. The ninth cause of action fails per Code of Civil Procedure section 1061 because the claim for declaratory relief is neither necessary nor proper. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CCP § 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA BEAN et al VS. TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 7, 2012, Line 22. DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with fifteen days leaves to amend. Plaintiffs' general and indefinite assertions of liability are not sufficient compliance with the rules of pleadings. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
